title: IntakeAnswers
description: Structured intake captured from a patient/user before it is handed to core/panel for a recommendation. Produced by any front-end (bot/, instanthpi/, epic/) and passed as the first argument to getRecommendation(). Contains no billing/EMR identifiers -- this is clinical-intake shape only.

intakeId.description: Opaque, front-end-generated id for correlating this intake with a later recommendation in logs. Not a patient identifier -- front-ends MUST NOT put PHI (name, health-card number, phone, address) in this field.

locale.description: BCP-47 language tag the intake was collected in and the recommendation should be produced in, e.g. 'en', 'fr-CA', 'sw', 'ht'.

jurisdiction.description: Optional ISO 3166-1/2 style region hint (e.g. 'CA-QC', 'KE', 'BD') used only to select jurisdiction-aware billing code suggestions downstream. Never required -- the patient-facing bot front-end should normally omit this entirely.

chiefComplaint.description: The primary reason for the encounter, in the patient's or clinician's own words.

onsetAndDuration.description: Free-text description of when the problem started and how it has evolved, if collected.

followUps.description: Ordered list of targeted follow-up question/answer pairs collected during the intake flow (e.g. HPI-style OPQRST, review-of-systems, or a track-specific questionnaire). May be empty only if chiefComplaint alone was collected (e.g. a very early triage gate).

question.description: The exact question text presented to the patient/user.

answer.description: The answer given. null means the question was asked but skipped/unknown/not applicable.

topic.description: Optional tag grouping this Q/A under a clinical topic (e.g. 'red-flag-screen', 'medication-history', 'ros-respiratory') to help panel/ weight relevance.

relevantHistory.description: Optional structured items the front-end already knows (e.g. pulled from an EMR by epic/, or previously given by a returning patient in bot/). Not required -- core/ must produce a usable recommendation with this array empty.

type.description: What kind of history item this is.

value.description: Free-text value, e.g. a condition name, a medication and dose, an allergy.

freeTextNotes.description: Any additional free-text the patient/user or front-end wants passed straight through to the panel, beyond the structured chiefComplaint/followUps. Optional.

ageRange.description: Coarse age bracket only, never a date of birth or exact age tied to identity. Front-ends SHOULD prefer a bracket over an exact age to reduce re-identification risk in logs.

sex.description: Biological sex, when clinically relevant and volunteered. Optional.
